senses.gloss: to act or be appointed (as a legal representative, agent, etc.)
to be responsible for, to vouch for, to guarantee

etymology.text: Compare وَكَلَ (wakala, “to trust, to rely on”). Appears to be related to Hebrew יכל (yakhól, “to be capable”). The definitions of both roots also appear to be conceptually related as trust is a product of perceived capability. From Proto-Semitic *wakal-.
